mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, it signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and economic expansion, driving positive forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint increases in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60d returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects resilient corporate output that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive short-term returns.
failure_condition: Rising industrial production during tightening yields negative 5d returns in > 65% of instances, or regime-specific IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI exceeds 190, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPI above 190 triggers positive 20d returns in > 60% of cases, or threshold model IC < 0.03 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates a strengthening labor market that boosts earnings expectations and equity prices.
failure_condition: Combined payroll growth and unemployment decline fail to predict positive 20d returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with increased corporate revenues and profits, producing positive forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth shows no significant correlation with 60d forward returns (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 115, it signals a severe supply-side slowdown and weakening economic activity, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO below 115 triggers positive 20d returns in > 60% of cases, or threshold signal IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year periods.
